rationale.R1.1: A section review centrally includes checking whether the thesis, motivation, and contribution framing are coherent at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: A review is expected to assess whether the argument moves logically from problem motivation to method, results, ablation, and broader claim.
rationale.R1.3: The section makes quantitative claims from reported results, so review centrally includes checking whether those claims are supported by the stated evidence.
rationale.R1.6: Reviewing a section normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: Any review projection must account for uncertainty about what can and cannot be established from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The section contains broad claims about physical priors and transfer to other PDE systems, making overclaim avoidance active.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation is only to review this section, so scope adherence is required to avoid expanding into editing or full-paper evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: Review responsibility includes avoiding advice or judgments that could mislead downstream scientific interpretation.
rationale.RX.5: The projection must preserve traceability to the delegation and artifact rather than introducing unsupported external assumptions.
